ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311737 covers samosa as a fried pastry food item; mini/cocktail size variant is not separately coded — exact match at genus level.
ontology.foodex2_description: Samosa — fried pastry with vegetable filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers samosa; vegetable/potato filling variant accepted as exact match at category level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for samosa as a food concept in the clinical terminology hierarchy; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 8 mini cocktail samosas weighing about 120g, typically served with tamarind chutney.

nutrient_source.title: Mini Veg Samosa — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id G00011045 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and provenance canonical. All per_100g macro and micronutrient values are taken directly from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for samosa (USDA SR Legacy #21052) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Samosa, vegetable-filled fried pastry — analogous estimate based on potato-filled fried pastry GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for mini vegetable samosa in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the closest analogous food: potato-filled fried pastry / samosa entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 50–60 for similar wheat-flour-encased potato-filled fried snacks. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (39.8g from 120g serving × 33.16g/100g) / 100 = 21.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 388mg/100g with low potassium offset (166mg/100g) and deep-fried saturated fat burden
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit mini vegetable samosa to 1–2 pieces per occasion, avoid high-sodium tamarind or chilli dipping sauces, and request a baked preparation where possible to reduce both sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (33.2g/100g) from wheat flour pastry and potato filling with low dietary fibre (1.8g/100g) producing a medium-to-high glycemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict portion to 1–2 mini samosas, pair with a high-fibre side such as salad or raita to blunt the glycemic response, and avoid repeat servings given the estimated glycemic load of approximately 22 per standard serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation contributing saturated fat (~3.2g/100g), cholesterol (14mg/100g), trace trans fat (0.1g/100g), and elevated sodium (388mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat mini vegetable samosa as an occasional food only, limiting to 1–2 pieces, choosing baked variants when available, and avoiding high-sodium accompaniments to reduce combined sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); potato and green chilli are nightshade vegetables that may aggravate inflammation; trace trans fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried samosas due to AGE formation and nightshade content; a baked version with non-nightshade filling is a preferable alternative if samosa-style snacks are desired.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mini Vegetable Samosa (also called Mini Veg Samosa or Cocktail Samosa) is a small deep-fried Indian pastry snack made from a thin wheat flour shell encasing a spiced filling of mashed potato, green peas, cumin, coriander, and green chilli. In Singapore, it is commonly served as a cocktail snack or appetiser in sets of 8 pieces accompanied by tamarind chutney. The exterior is golden-brown, crisp, and flaky from deep-frying in vegetable oil; the interior is soft, mildly spiced, and starchy. Per 100g, it provides 310 kcal, 5.1g protein, 17.5g fat, 33.2g carbohydrate, 1.8g dietary fibre, and 388mg sodium. The high carbohydrate content from refined wheat flour and potato, combined with low fibre and deep-fried fat, makes this a high-concern food for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low) based on analogous potato-filled fried pastry data. It is vegetarian, vegan, and halal, but contains gluten. Culturally, samosas are a staple Indian street food and party snack widely adopted across Singapore's multicultural food landscape.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a mini samosa?
Can I eat samosa if I have high blood pressure?
How much sodium is in a samosa?
What is the GI of samosa?
How many carbs in a samosa?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record (cr_id G00011045, confidence 0.95, provenance canonical, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy closest match and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food entry and documented. All mandatory multilingual names present for Indian/Singaporean cuisine category.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.2g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for samosa (closest approved-source analogue) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this item. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for this specific item. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail not reported in HPB Foodo for this item; USDA SR Legacy does not have a sufficiently close match to justify imputation. 5. trans_fat_g set to 0.1g/100g based on known trace trans fat formation during deep-frying in vegetable oil; this is consistent with the input health_suitability notes. 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database potato-filled fried pastry data; no direct samosa GI entry was found. 7. Glycemic load per serving = 55 × (120g × 0.3316) / 100 = 55 × 39.79 / 100 = 21.9. 8. All per_100g core macros (protein_g 5.14, fat_g 17.47, carbohydrate_g 33.16) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
